stations.name: Coorparoo railway station
stations.walk_minutes_note: Within the suburb. Most streets are within a 10 to 15 minute walk.

trains.approx_cbd_minutes_offpeak_note: Approximately 10 to 14 minutes to Central station off-peak.

buses.note: Bus routes serve Coorparoo via Old Cleveland Road and Cavendish Road.

driving.motorway_access: M3 corridor for southern travel. Story Bridge for the CBD via Stanley Street.
driving.approx_cbd_minutes_offpeak_note: Approximately 9 to 13 minutes to Brisbane CBD off-peak.

lifestyle.shops_dining_note: Coorparoo Square is the major retail centre with cinema, supermarket, and casual dining. Old Cleveland Road has a string of cafes and bars. Stones Corner precinct is a short drive for additional retail.

pockets.name: Station and Square precinct
pockets.character: Streets close to Coorparoo station and Coorparoo Square. Mix of apartments, townhouses, and character homes. Walkability and transport are the proposition.

pockets.name: Family streets
pockets.character: Quieter residential streets with established character home stock. Larger blocks.

pockets.name: Cavendish Road corridor
pockets.character: Streets along the Cavendish Road arterial. Convenient for transport but trades on a different value proposition.

buyer_profile.note: Buyers moving from Greenslopes or Holland Park for the train station and the Coorparoo Square precinct.
Apartments and townhouses around Coorparoo Square offer accessible inner-east entry points.
Train station and arterial proximity supports rental demand.
The character home market on quieter streets attracts family buyers targeting the catchment corridor.
